hypothesis: PHP syntax errors and layout structure regressions are now gated by prior keeps. The remaining unaddressed failure class is WP runtime errors: undefined functions, missing hooks, and PHP fatals that WP renders as styled error pages. These PASS the current structural marker check (header/footer/main/nav are still present in WP's critical-error template) while signaling a broken deploy. Adding a body error-string scan on all 6 smoke URLs will catch: 'Fatal error', 'Parse error', 'There has been a critical error', 'Call to undefined', 'Call to a member function', 'class not found'. Basis: the 7 failures on 2026-04-22 were runtime class (seeding failures, undefined method calls, WP hook issues) — php -l missed them; structural marker check also misses them since WP error pages render layout. This is the explicit next-candidate gap noted in the current surface. Both prior gate-layers were surgical one-class fixes that kept at 100%; this follows the same pattern for the next identified class.
changes_description: Added WP runtime error body scan (step 4) to the pre-push checklist. Scans all 6 SMOKE_URLS for 7 error string patterns: 'Fatal error', 'Parse error', 'There has been a critical error', 'Call to undefined', 'Call to a member function', 'class not found', 'wp-die'. Inserted between structural marker check (step 3) and HTTP smoke test (step 5). Surface title updated. Known gaps section updated to reflect current state.
